title: Mergen verification report
description: Machine-readable record of an independent verification pass. Companion to verification-report.md. Each task carries a confidence label so calibration is recorded, not assumed.

verified_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the verification pass

risk_triggers.description: The Governor floor trigger ids that classified this report's risk_level (for example auth-path, secrets-path). Empty when the change touched no guarded surface. Records why the tier is what it is.

total_done_tasks.description: Count of tasks marked done that the harness verified.

mechanically_passed.description: Done tasks a mechanical lens confirmed as a pass.

mechanically_failed.description: Done tasks a mechanical lens confirmed as a fail (a phantom completion).

ambiguous.description: Done tasks no mechanical lens could decide, neither passed nor failed.

untested_passes.description: Verified passes whose strongest evidence was a static lens, no test executed (evidence_tier corroborated).

human_review.description: Machine-readable human sign-off state. When human_review_required is true, a strict gate requires status approved before a final pass.

approved_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of approval, or null

then.description: An approval is only real when it records who approved, when, and on what evidence. A bare {status: approved} is not a sign-off, the same way a high-trust report is not a pass until it is signed.

A high-trust report must at minimum flag that human review is required. The flag is then honoured by a recorded human_review approval before a final pass, the same non-downgradable invariant the Governor decision carries for its tier.

confidence.description: How the verdict is known, from the one confidence vocabulary defined in MERGEN_PRINCIPLES.md and mirrored by verify_core.CONFIDENCE. extracted means direct filesystem or test evidence. A verdict without evidence is fail, never an inferred pass.

evidence_strength.description: Optional calibration. The share of total lens weight that returned a pass for this task, a deterministic ranking of how strongly the passing evidence grounds the verdict. It is not a calibrated probability and it never changes the verdict.

evidence_tier.description: Optional calibration. The strongest passing evidence class: executed (a test ran and passed), corroborated (a static lens passed but no test did), or none (no lens passed). Describes corroboration, not the verdict.

then.description: A pass must carry concrete evidence and cannot be ambiguous. A verdict without evidence is a fail, never an inferred or ambiguous pass.

provenance.description: What produced this report, so tampering or staleness is detectable. Written by verify_core.compute_provenance. The report bytes are also hashed into a <report>.sha256 sidecar; verify_core --check-manifest recomputes and compares.

source_commit.description: git HEAD of the verified root, or null outside a git work tree

working_tree_clean.description: whether the verified root had no uncommitted changes, or null outside a git work tree

tasks_state_sha256.description: sha256 of the exact tasks-state file bytes the report verified
